next_step_text: 100% Confidence 1st Jun KIV and waiting on budget validation internally- Reach out this week, and f2F 12th Jun to update status 13th Mar ck sent follow up email to arrange call asap to work through any blockers 6th Feb - shared commercials for Mgmt sign off 23rd Jan - Fit Gap and Business case presented 20th Jan- final workshop (B'Case/ROI on Fri 23rd F2F to get sign off with mgmt by 30th Jan- non

our_open_deliverables.text: Follow up with Sherald Yap and Akshay Dhanda in the coming weeks regarding invoicing options and efficiency improvements
Send follow-up email outlining proposed focus areas for next session and request bullet points from Gamuda on key topics and challenges
Provide Zycus product roadmap detailing Google Workspace migration support and integration timeline (in response to Bharti's question on GWS compatibility)

buyer_dependent_items.text: Sherald Yap to send general landscape overview of current Alibaba integration, including prerequisites and required preparations for upcoming technical sessions
Gamuda to send 5–6 bullet points of key topics and challenges to drill down into for the next meeting
EXCO budget validation and approval decision (business case presented 23 Jan, approval planned by 30 Jan, outcome pending as of 1 June)

competitive_position.summary: Non-competitive on vendors; the live threat is status-quo inertia. The strongest current threat is Gamuda deferring the $300k because EXCO must justify replacing a functional, already-seven-figure Alibaba + SAP S/4HANA/Ariba stack. Win by quantifying the agentic-AI savings on tail/low-risk spend the incumbent cannot deliver.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo (Alibaba network + SAP S/4HANA incumbent stack)
competitors.quote: we also need to justify why the current system can't do it. We need to spend another million dollars to buy a new one.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify agentic AI savings on tail/low-risk spend and centralisation efficiency the incumbent cannot deliver; arm Sherald with a CFO-ready ROI case.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent procurement layer)
competitors.quote: we'd love you to throw everything to do with Ariba away. We would love for you to do that.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 19 Nov 2025 (Carl Kimball / Akshay Dhanda)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin one-platform agentic source-to-pay and certified S/4HANA adapters as a superior replacement for the bolt-on Ariba layer, while supporting a hybrid co-existence path.

champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 19 Nov 2025; Next_Step__c ('strong leaning to Zycus', 'non competitive')
champion_strength.summary: Sherald Yap drove the deal from intro through business case with strong leaning to Zycus, but the ~5-month EXCO stall and ~109-day buyer-email silence suggest she may lack power to unblock the budget decision.

sources: Next_Step__c (1 Jun budget validation; 23 Jan mgmt sign-off)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The budget decision sits with EXCO and is the live blocker: Next_Step__c shows the business case went to management for sign-off (23 Jan 2026) and as of 1 Jun 2026 the deal is 'waiting on budget validation internally.' On the 19 Nov call Sherald referenced needing top-management alignment and that a CFO would demand 'a business case ... an ROI.' But no individual economic buyer (CFO or EXCO sponsor) is mapped as an engaged contact role, and none appears as a named speaker on any read call. Sherald is a mid-level Procurement Manager and cannot clear an EXCO budget decision alone — reaching the budget owner, brokered through Sherald, is the single highest-leverage gap to close.

recommended_moves: George calls Sherald (phone, not email) to diagnose what is actually blocking the EXCO budget decision — is it ROI, cost vs. the existing stack, a competing priority, or a deferral — and confirm whether it is still funded for FY.

Co-build a CFO-ready, quantified ROI/savings model with Sherald — tail/low-risk (fuel) spend automation savings, PR cycle-time reduction, and the cost of the decentralised-to-centralised shift — to replace the qualitative case that is fuelling status-quo inertia.

Arm Sherald to broker a working session with the EXCO/CFO budget owner; add the buyer-side contacts (Sherald, Loo Yee Mei, Joey Lye, Bharti Vadivelu) as opportunity contact roles so the deal is not single-threaded on one quiet champion. Where Sherald cannot open the door, offer a Zycus VP peer-to-peer to support her (deal is Best Case / forecasted).
